SUPERAPP FRAMEWORK — LLM REFERENCE
====================================

Teelm is a TypeScript web framework implementing The Elm Architecture (TEA).
Zero runtime dependencies. Uses Bun as runtime and Vite as bundler.

Package: "teelm"
Subpath exports:
  "teelm"              — core (app, h, noFx, withFx, mapCmd, types)
  "teelm/fx"           — effects (http, delay, navigate, storageGet/Set, log, dispatchMsg)
  "teelm/subs"         — subscriptions (interval, onKeyDown, websocket, ...)
  "teelm/router"       — typed routing + Page protocol
  "teelm/functional"   — Result, Maybe, Decoder, HttpError, branded Url/Path/RouteName
  "teelm/task"         — Task<E, T> for composable async
  "teelm/events"       — typed event helpers (makeEvents)
  "teelm/testing"      — test helpers
  "teelm/debugger"     — time-travel overlay
  "teelm/jsx-runtime"  — JSX automatic transform

TypeScript: strict, exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true.

================================================================================
CORE INVARIANTS — read first
================================================================================

1. init and update are TUPLE-ONLY: readonly [State, Cmd<Msg>].
   Use noFx(state) when there are no effects. There is NO bare-state shorthand.

2. Cmd<Msg> and Sub<Msg> are BRANDED types. The only way to construct them is
   via the public helpers (none, withFx, batch, mapCmd, mapEffect, and the
   sub creators in teelm/subs). A raw [fn, props] tuple does NOT satisfy
   Cmd<Msg> or Sub<Msg> at the type level.

3. subscriptions(state) returns Subs<Msg> = readonly (Sub<Msg> | false | null | undefined)[].
   Falsy entries are dropped at runtime — perfect for `cond && interval(...)`.

4. State is DEEP-FROZEN on every transition by default. Opt out with
   `freezeState: false` in AppConfig only for known hot paths.

5. To tear down an app, call `instance.destroy()`. There is no "return null
   from update to destroy" magic.

6. http() and storageGet() are decoder-based. They surface a Result<T, HttpError>
   (resp. Result<T | undefined, StorageError | string>) to your update function.
   Use Decode.* from teelm/functional to build decoders.

================================================================================
CORE TYPES (teelm)
================================================================================

VNode — Virtual DOM node
  { tag: string | MemoView, props: Record<string, any>, key: string | number | undefined,
    children: VNode[], type: number, memo: unknown, node: Node | undefined }

MemoView<P> = (props: P) => VNode
Dispatch<Msg> = (msg: Msg | readonly Msg[]) => void

EffectFn<Msg, P = any> = (dispatch: Dispatch<Msg>, props: P) => void
Effect<Msg, P = any>   = readonly [EffectFn<Msg, P>, P]

// Branded — only constructable via none / withFx / batch / mapCmd / mapEffect.
Cmd<Msg> = readonly Effect<Msg>[] & { readonly [__cmdBrand]: Msg }

SubFn<Msg, P = any> = (dispatch: Dispatch<Msg>, props: P) => () => void
// Branded — only constructable via the sub creators in teelm/subs.
Sub<Msg, P = any>   = readonly [SubFn<Msg, P>, P] & { readonly [__subBrand]: Msg }

// Subscription list, allows falsy entries.
Subs<Msg> = readonly (Sub<Msg> | false | null | undefined)[]

// init / update are tuple-only.
UpdateResult<S, Msg> = readonly [S, Cmd<Msg>]
Update<S, Msg>       = (state: Readonly<S>, msg: Msg) => UpdateResult<S, Msg>
Init<S, Msg>         = readonly [S, Cmd<Msg>]

AppConfig<S, Msg>:
  init: Init<S, Msg>
  update: Update<S, Msg>
  view: (state: Readonly<S>, dispatch: Dispatch<Msg>) => VNode
  subscriptions?: (state: Readonly<S>) => Subs<Msg>
  node: HTMLElement
  onMount?: (hook: { state, dispatch, node }) => void
  afterRender?: (hook: { state, prevState, dispatch, node }) => void
  onUnmount?: (hook: { state, node }) => void
  debug?: boolean | DebugConfig
  middleware?: (dispatch: Dispatch<Msg>) => Dispatch<Msg>
  freezeState?: boolean   // default true — deep-freeze state on every transition

DebugConfig:
  console?: boolean
  history?: boolean
  maxHistory?: number     // default 200

AppInstance<S, Msg>:
  dispatch: Dispatch<Msg>
  getState: () => Readonly<S>
  destroy: () => void
  getHistory: () => readonly Readonly<S>[]
  getHistoryIndex: () => number
  goBack: () => void
  goForward: () => void
  jumpTo: (index: number) => void

================================================================================
CORE FUNCTIONS (teelm)
================================================================================

h(tag: string, props?: Record<string, any> | null, ...children: any[]): VNode
  Create a VNode. Props support: class (string | array | object), className, style
  (string | object), onXxx event handlers, ref, key.

text(value: string | number | boolean): VNode
  Create a text VNode.

memo<P>(component: MemoView<P>, props: P): VNode
  Memoized component. Skips re-render if props are shallowly equal.

lazy<T>(view: (data: T) => VNode, data: T): VNode
  Lazy evaluation; same shape as memo.

app<S, Msg>(config: AppConfig<S, Msg>): AppInstance<S, Msg>
  Mount an application. Starts the TEA cycle. State is deep-frozen on every
  transition unless freezeState: false.

noFx<S>(state: S): readonly [S, Cmd<never>]
  Wrap state with no effects. The Cmd<never> result satisfies any Cmd<Msg>.

withFx<S, Msg>(state: S, ...effects: readonly Effect<Msg>[]): readonly [S, Cmd<Msg>]
  Wrap state with one or more effects.

batch<Msg>(commands: readonly Cmd<Msg>[]): Cmd<Msg>
  Merge multiple Cmds into one.

none: Cmd<never>
  Empty Cmd, polymorphic in Msg.

mapEffect<A, B, P>(effect: Effect<A, P>, fn: (a: A) => B): Effect<B, P>
  Lift one effect into a parent Msg space.

mapCmd<A, B>(cmd: Cmd<A>, fn: (a: A) => B): Cmd<B>
  Lift every effect inside a Cmd into a parent Msg space. Use this in nested
  TEA when you receive a child's Cmd from the child's update.

mapSub<A, B, P>(sub: Sub<A, P> | false | null | undefined, fn): Sub<B, P> | false | null | undefined
  Lift a subscription's Msg type. Passes falsy values through.

mapDispatch<A, B>(dispatch: Dispatch<B>, fn: (a: A) => B): Dispatch<A>
  Wrap a dispatch so a child can call it with its own Msg type.

batchSubs<Msg>(...args: (Subs<Msg> | Sub<Msg> | false | null | undefined)[]): Subs<Msg>
  Flatten subscription lists from multiple sources into one Subs<Msg>.

resolveClass(value: unknown): string
  Resolve class values: string, array (falsy filtered), object (truthy keys joined).

================================================================================
EFFECTS (teelm/fx)
================================================================================

--- HTTP ---

http<T, Msg>(props: {
  url: string;
  options?: RequestInit;
  expect?: "json" | "text";          // default "json"
  decoder: Decoder<T>;                // validates the body
  timeoutMs?: number;
  toMsg: (result: Result<T, HttpError>) => Msg;
}): Effect<Msg>

  Fetch and decode. expect: "json" parses+validates with `decoder`. expect: "text"
  passes the raw body string through `decoder` (so a decoder receiving `unknown`
  is still required — typically Decode.string for plain text).
  All failures (BadUrl, Timeout, NetworkError, BadStatus, BadBody) are surfaced
  as Result.err with a typed HttpError.

  Example:
    http({
      url: "/api/users",
      decoder: Decode.array(Decode.object({ id: Decode.number, name: Decode.string })),
      toMsg: (r) => r.tag === "Ok"
        ? { tag: "GotUsers", users: r.value }
        : { tag: "FetchFailed", error: r.error },
    })

--- delay ---

delay<Msg>(ms: number, msg: Msg): Effect<Msg>
  Dispatch msg after ms milliseconds.

--- navigate ---

navigate<Msg>(url: string, replace?: boolean): Effect<Msg>
  Push (or replace) browser history. Dispatches a popstate event so the router
  picks up the change.

--- storageSet ---

storageSet<Msg>(
  key: string,
  value: string,
  toMsg?: (result: Result<undefined, StorageError>) => Msg,
): Effect<Msg>

  Write to localStorage. If toMsg is provided, dispatches a Result message
  (Ok with undefined, or Err with a typed StorageError). Without toMsg the
  call is fire-and-forget (errors swallowed) — the explicit Result form is
  recommended for production code.

--- storageGet ---

storageGet<T, Msg>(props: {
  key: string;
  decoder: Decoder<T>;
  json?: boolean;                                    // default true: JSON.parse before decoding
  toMsg: (result: Result<T | undefined, StorageError | string>) => Msg;
}): Effect<Msg>

  Read from localStorage and decode. Missing key dispatches Result.ok(undefined).
  JSON parse failures dispatch Result.err(<string reason>). Decoder failures
  dispatch Result.err(<decoder error string>). DOM-level errors dispatch
  Result.err(<StorageError>).

--- StorageError ---

type StorageError =
  | { tag: "QuotaExceeded"; message: string }
  | { tag: "SecurityError"; message: string }
  | { tag: "Unavailable";   message: string }
  | { tag: "Unknown";       message: string }

--- log / dispatchMsg / compactEffects ---

log<Msg>(...args: unknown[]): Effect<Msg>
  console.log as an effect. Useful for tracing dispatch flow.

dispatchMsg<Msg>(msg: Msg): Effect<Msg>
  Dispatch a message as an effect. Useful inside batched effect lists.

compactEffects<Msg>(...effects: (Effect<Msg> | false | null | undefined)[]): Effect<Msg>[]
  Filter out falsy effects before passing to withFx via spread.

================================================================================
SUBSCRIPTIONS (teelm/subs)
================================================================================

interval<Msg>(ms: number, msg: Msg | ((now: number) => Msg)): Sub<Msg>
onKeyDown<Msg>(msg: (key: string, e: KeyboardEvent) => Msg): Sub<Msg>
onKeyUp<Msg>(msg: (key: string, e: KeyboardEvent) => Msg): Sub<Msg>
onMouseMove<Msg>(msg: (x: number, y: number) => Msg): Sub<Msg>
onResize<Msg>(msg: (w: number, h: number) => Msg): Sub<Msg>
onUrlChange<Msg>(msg: (url: URL) => Msg): Sub<Msg>
onAnimationFrame<Msg>(msg: (timestamp: number) => Msg): Sub<Msg>
onEvent<Msg>(event: string, msg: (e: Event) => Msg, target?: EventTarget): Sub<Msg>
websocket<Msg>(props: {
  url: string;
  protocols?: string | string[];
  onMessage: (data: unknown) => Msg;
  onOpen?: () => Msg;
  onClose?: () => Msg;
  onError?: (e: Event) => Msg;
}): Sub<Msg>

These are the ONLY way to construct a Sub<Msg>. To make a custom subscription,
write a SubFn and brand the tuple via one of the helpers (see "Custom
subscription" pattern below).

================================================================================
FUNCTIONAL UTILITIES (teelm/functional)
================================================================================

--- Maybe<T> ---

type Maybe<T> = { tag: "Just"; value: T } | { tag: "Nothing" }

Maybe.just(value)               : Maybe<T>
Maybe.nothing()                 : Maybe<never>
Maybe.map(maybe, fn)            : Maybe<U>
Maybe.andThen(maybe, fn)        : Maybe<U>
Maybe.withDefault(maybe, def)   : T
Maybe.fromNullable(value)       : Maybe<T>           // null/undefined → Nothing

--- Result<T, E> ---

type Result<T, E> = { tag: "Ok"; value: T } | { tag: "Err"; error: E }

Result.ok(value)                : Result<T, E>
Result.err(error)               : Result<T, E>
Result.map(result, fn)          : Result<U, E>
Result.mapError(result, fn)     : Result<T, F>
Result.andThen(result, fn)      : Result<U, E>
Result.withDefault(result, def) : T
Result.toMaybe(result)          : Maybe<T>

--- Decoder<T> ---

type Decoder<T> = (raw: unknown) => Result<T, string>

Decode.string                       : Decoder<string>
Decode.number                       : Decoder<number>          // finite numbers only
Decode.boolean                      : Decoder<boolean>
Decode.null                         : Decoder<null>
Decode.unknown                      : Decoder<unknown>
Decode.array(item)                  : Decoder<T[]>
Decode.field(key, inner)            : Decoder<T>               // pluck one field
Decode.optional(inner)              : Decoder<T | undefined>   // null/undefined → Ok(undefined)
Decode.oneOf(...decoders)           : Decoder<T>               // first that succeeds
Decode.object({ k1: d1, k2: d2 })   : Decoder<{ k1: T1, k2: T2 }>
Decode.map(inner, fn)               : Decoder<U>
Decode.andThen(inner, fn)           : Decoder<U>
Decode.fromJsonString(decoder)      : (text: string) => Result<T, string>

Example:
  const userDecoder = Decode.object({
    id: Decode.number,
    name: Decode.string,
    email: Decode.optional(Decode.string),
  });

--- HttpError ---

type HttpError =
  | { tag: "BadUrl";       url: string }
  | { tag: "Timeout" }
  | { tag: "NetworkError"; message: string }
  | { tag: "BadStatus";    status: number; statusText: string; body: string }
  | { tag: "BadBody";      reason: string; body: string }

HttpError.badUrl(url)
HttpError.timeout()
HttpError.networkError(message)
HttpError.badStatus(status, statusText, body)
HttpError.badBody(reason, body)
HttpError.toString(err): string

--- Branded Url / Path / RouteName ---

type Url       = Opaque<string, "Url">
type Path      = Opaque<string, "Path">
type RouteName = Opaque<string, "RouteName">

Url.parse(raw: string): Maybe<Url>      // throws → Nothing on invalid
Url.fromString(raw: string): Url         // unchecked cast — only when known-valid
Url.toString(u: Url): string
Url.isAbsolute(raw: string): boolean

Path.parse(raw: string): Maybe<Path>    // empty string → Nothing; normalizes leading "/"
Path.fromString(raw: string): Path       // normalizes leading "/"
Path.toString(p: Path): string

RouteName.fromString(raw: string): RouteName
RouteName.toString(n: RouteName): string

--- Generic Opaque / brand ---

type Opaque<T, K extends string> = T & { readonly [__brand]: K }
brand<T, K extends string>(val: T): Opaque<T, K>

--- pipe ---

pipe(x, f1, f2?, f3?, f4?)
  Left-to-right function composition. Up to 4 functions typed; more accepted at runtime.

================================================================================
TASKS (teelm/task)
================================================================================

A Task<E, T> describes lazy async work that may succeed with T or fail with E.
Tasks compose; you don't need an intermediate Msg variant per step in a chain.

type Task<E, T> = () => Promise<Result<T, E>>

Task.succeed<T, E = never>(value: T): Task<E, T>
Task.fail<E, T = never>(error: E): Task<E, T>
Task.fromTry<E, T>(fn: () => T, onError: (e: unknown) => E): Task<E, T>
Task.fromPromise<E, T>(create: () => Promise<T>, onError: (e: unknown) => E): Task<E, T>
Task.fromPromiseResult<E, T>(create: () => Promise<Result<T, E>>): Task<E, T>

Task.map<E, T, U>(task, fn: (t: T) => U): Task<E, U>
Task.mapError<E, F, T>(task, fn: (e: E) => F): Task<F, T>
Task.andThen<E, T, U>(task, fn: (t: T) => Task<E, U>): Task<E, U>
Task.onError<E, F, T>(task, recover: (e: E) => Task<F, T>): Task<F, T>

Task.sequence<E, T>(tasks: readonly Task<E, T>[]): Task<E, T[]>      // serial
Task.all<E, T>(tasks: readonly Task<E, T>[]): Task<E, T[]>           // parallel

// Convert to Cmd<Msg>:
Task.attempt<E, T, Msg>(task: Task<E, T>, toMsg: (r: Result<T, E>) => Msg): Effect<Msg>
Task.perform<T, Msg>(task: Task<never, T>, toMsg: (v: T) => Msg): Effect<Msg>

Example — sequenced auth + fetch with one final Msg:
  const fetchAuth = Task.fromPromise(
    () => fetch("/api/auth").then(r => r.json() as Promise<{ token: string }>),
    (e) => ({ tag: "AuthFailed" as const, message: String(e) }),
  );
  const fetchUser = (token: string) => Task.fromPromise(
    () => fetch(`/api/me?t=${token}`).then(r => r.json() as Promise<User>),
    (e) => ({ tag: "UserFailed" as const, message: String(e) }),
  );
  const loadCurrentUser = Task.andThen(fetchAuth, ({ token }) => fetchUser(token));

  // In update:
  return withFx(state, Task.attempt(loadCurrentUser, (r) => ({ tag: "GotUser", r })));

================================================================================
TYPED EVENT HELPERS (teelm/events)
================================================================================

makeEvents<Msg>(dispatch: Dispatch<Msg>): Events<Msg>

  Returns an object with handlers that dispatch via the captured `dispatch`.
  Every method returns a partial props object suitable for spreading onto a VNode.

  E.onClick(msg)            -> { onClick: (e) => dispatch(msg) }
  E.onClickWith(fn)         -> { onClick: (e) => maybeDispatch(fn(e)) }
  E.onDoubleClick(msg)      -> { onDblclick: ... }
  E.onInput(fn)             -> { onInput:  (e) => maybeDispatch(fn(target.value)) }
  E.onChange(fn)            -> { onChange: (e) => maybeDispatch(fn(target.value)) }
  E.onChecked(fn)           -> { onChange: (e) => maybeDispatch(fn(target.checked)) }
  E.onSubmit(msg)           -> { onSubmit: (e) => { e.preventDefault(); dispatch(msg) } }
  E.onSubmitWith(fn)        -> { onSubmit: (e) => { preventDefault; maybeDispatch(fn(new FormData(form))) } }
  E.onKeyDown(fn) / onKeyUp(fn)  -> { onKeydown / onKeyup: ... }
  E.onEnter(msg) / onEscape(msg) -> { onKeydown: only-on-Enter / only-on-Escape }
  E.onFocus(msg) / onBlur(msg)   -> { onFocus / onBlur: ... }

  `fn`-style handlers (onClickWith, onInput, onChange, onChecked, onSubmitWith,
  onKeyDown, onKeyUp) may return undefined to skip dispatch.

Example:
  function view(state: State, dispatch: Dispatch<Msg>) {
    const E = makeEvents(dispatch);
    return h("form", E.onSubmit({ tag: "Save" }),
      h("input", { type: "text", value: state.name, ...E.onInput((v) => ({ tag: "SetName", value: v })) }),
      h("button", { type: "submit" }, "Save"),
    );
  }

================================================================================
TESTING (teelm/testing)
================================================================================

getModel<S, Msg>(result: UpdateResult<S, Msg>): S
getEffects<S, Msg>(result: UpdateResult<S, Msg>): Cmd<Msg>
hasEffects<S, Msg>(result: UpdateResult<S, Msg>): boolean
runEffect<Msg, P>(effect: Effect<Msg, P>, dispatch: Dispatch<Msg>): void
runCmd<Msg>(cmd: Cmd<Msg>, dispatch: Dispatch<Msg>): void
createDispatchSpy<Msg>(): { dispatch, messages: Msg[], last(): Msg | undefined, clear(): void }

Example:
  const spy = createDispatchSpy<Msg>();
  const [next, cmd] = update(state, { tag: "Save" });
  runCmd(cmd, spy.dispatch);
  expect(spy.last()).toEqual({ tag: "Saved" });

================================================================================
ROUTER (teelm/router)
================================================================================

--- URL parsers ---

Parser<T>:      { _tag: "path";  parse: (raw: string) => T | null }
QueryParser<T>: { _tag: "query"; parse: (raw: string | null) => T }

str:    Parser<string>    — any string segment
int:    Parser<number>    — integer segment
float:  Parser<number>    — finite number segment
oneOf<T extends string>(values: readonly T[]): Parser<T>

q.str(fallback: string):       QueryParser<string>
q.int(fallback: number):       QueryParser<number>
q.float(fallback: number):     QueryParser<number>
q.bool(fallback: boolean):     QueryParser<boolean>      // "true"/"1" → true
q.optional.str():              QueryParser<string | undefined>
q.optional.int():              QueryParser<number | undefined>

--- Route definition ---

RouteDef<Params>:
  pattern: string
  parse(pathname, search?): Params | null
  toUrl(params: Params): string

route(path: string): RouteDef<{}>
route<Spec>(path: string, spec: Spec): RouteDef<ExtractParams<Spec>>

Pattern syntax:
  "/users"          static segment
  "/users/:id"      dynamic segment (key in spec)
  "/docs/*"         wildcard, captured under "*" key

Examples:
  route("/")
  route("/users/:id", { id: int })
  route("/search", { q: q.str(""), page: q.int(1) })
  route("/users/:id/posts", { id: int, page: q.int(1) })

router.href(routeDef, params) returns a string. If you need a branded value,
wrap with Path.fromString(...).

--- Page protocol ---

PageConfig<Model, Msg, Shared, Params>:
  init(params: Params, shared: Shared): readonly [Model, Cmd<Msg>]
  update(model: Readonly<Model>, msg: Msg, shared: Readonly<Shared>): readonly [Model, Cmd<Msg>]
  view(model: Readonly<Model>, shared: Readonly<Shared>, dispatch: Dispatch<Msg>): VNode
  subscriptions?(model, shared): Subs<Msg>
  save?(model: Readonly<Model>): unknown
  load?(saved: unknown, params: Params, shared: Shared): readonly [Model, Cmd<Msg>]
  onMount?(ctx): void
  onUnmount?(ctx): void
  afterUpdate?(ctx): void
  onError?(ctx): void
  errorView?(ctx): VNode

init / update / load all return tuples — use noFx(model) when there are no effects.

Type parameters:
  Model  — page-local state
  Msg    — page-local message type
  Shared — app-wide shared state (read-only inside the page)
  Params — URL parameters from the route parser

Lifecycle:
  Navigate to page    → init (or load if cached)
  User interaction    → update
  Render              → view (or errorView on view crash)
  Events              → subscriptions
  Navigate away       → save (caches model if returns non-undefined)
  Return to page      → load (restores from cache)
  DOM mounted         → onMount(ctx)
  DOM updated         → afterUpdate(ctx)        // including shared changes
  DOM removed         → onUnmount(ctx)
  Lifecycle hook throws → onError(ctx) with phase
  view throws          → onError + errorView (or default fallback)

PageMountContext: { model, params, shared, dispatch, root: HTMLElement }
PageAfterUpdateContext extends PageMountContext + prevModel/prevParams/prevShared
PageErrorContext: PageRenderContext + { phase, error, root?, prev*? }

--- Page binding ---

PageRoute<Shared> = binding of RouteDef + PageConfig + optional guard.

page<Model, Msg, Shared, Params>(
  routeDef: RouteDef<Params>,
  config: PageConfig<Model, Msg, Shared, Params>,
  options?: { guard?: (params: Params, shared: Readonly<Shared>) => true | string },
): PageRoute<Shared>

Guard: return true to allow, or a redirect URL string. Guards chain up to 5
levels deep before falling through to notFound.

--- Router instance ---

createRouter<Shared>(config: {
  routes: PageRoute<Shared>[];
  shared: Shared;
  notFound?: PageConfig<any, any, Shared, { path: string }>;
}): Router<Shared>

Router<Shared>:
  init(url?: URL): readonly [RouterModel<Shared>, Cmd<RouterMsg<Shared>>]
  update(model, msg): readonly [RouterModel<Shared>, Cmd<RouterMsg<Shared>>]
  view(model, dispatch): VNode
  subscriptions(model): Subs<RouterMsg<Shared>>
  listen(): Sub<RouterMsg<Shared>>                  // popstate, dispatches initial UrlChanged
  navigate(url, replace?): Effect<RouterMsg<Shared>>
  updateShared(fn: (s: Shared) => Shared): RouterMsg<Shared>
  href(routeDef, params): string

RouterMsg<Shared>:
  | { tag: "@@router/UrlChanged"; url: URL }
  | { tag: "@@router/PageMsg"; msg: unknown }
  | { tag: "@@router/UpdateShared"; fn: (s: Shared) => Shared }

RouterModel<Shared>:
  shared: Shared
  url: URL
  _page: { key, model, routeIdx, params } | null
  _cache: Map<string, unknown>

--- routerApp() — zero-boilerplate ---

routerApp<Shared>(config: {
  router: Router<Shared>;
  layout: (content: VNode, shared: Readonly<Shared>, dispatch: Dispatch<RouterMsg<Shared>>) => VNode;
  node: HTMLElement;
  url?: URL;            // bootstrap from a deterministic URL (testing, SSR-like)
  listen?: boolean;     // default true; false disables popstate listener
  debug?: boolean | DebugConfig;
}): AppInstance<{ router: RouterModel<Shared> }, RouterMsg<Shared>>

--- routerLink ---

routerLink(url: string): { href: string; onClick: (e: MouseEvent) => void }
  SPA navigation. Spread onto an <a>. Respects modifier keys (Ctrl/Meta/Shift +
  click opens new tab). Uses pushState + popstate.

================================================================================
DEBUGGER (teelm/debugger)
================================================================================

DebuggerConfig:
  position?: "bottom-right" | "bottom-left" | "top-right" | "top-left"
  collapsed?: boolean
  width?: string

attachDebugger<S, Msg>(instance: AppInstance<S, Msg>, config?: DebuggerConfig): { destroy: () => void }
  Floating overlay with state inspection and time-travel controls. Requires
  debug: true (or { history: true }) on the app instance.

================================================================================
JSX RUNTIME (teelm/jsx-runtime)
================================================================================

tsconfig.json:
  "jsx": "react-jsx"
  "jsxImportSource": "teelm"

jsx(tag, props): VNode
jsxs — alias for jsx
jsxDEV — alias for jsx
Fragment({ children? }): VNode    // empty-string tag; renders children inline

================================================================================
COMMON PATTERNS
================================================================================

--- Basic TEA app ---

import { app, h, noFx, type Dispatch, type Init, type UpdateResult } from "teelm";

interface State { count: number }
type Msg = { tag: "Inc" } | { tag: "Dec" };

const init: Init<State, Msg> = noFx({ count: 0 });

function update(state: State, msg: Msg): UpdateResult<State, Msg> {
  switch (msg.tag) {
    case "Inc": return noFx({ ...state, count: state.count + 1 });
    case "Dec": return noFx({ ...state, count: state.count - 1 });
  }
}

function view(state: State, dispatch: Dispatch<Msg>) {
  return h("div", {},
    h("button", { onClick: () => dispatch({ tag: "Dec" }) }, "-"),
    h("span", {}, String(state.count)),
    h("button", { onClick: () => dispatch({ tag: "Inc" }) }, "+"),
  );
}

app({ init, update, view, node: document.getElementById("app")! });

--- Effects from update ---

import { withFx, noFx } from "teelm";
import { http, delay, storageSet, storageGet } from "teelm/fx";
import { Decode } from "teelm/functional";

const userDecoder = Decode.object({ id: Decode.number, name: Decode.string });

function update(state: State, msg: Msg) {
  switch (msg.tag) {
    case "DelayedInc":
      return withFx(state, delay(500, { tag: "Inc" }));

    case "FetchUser":
      return withFx(
        { ...state, loading: true },
        http({
          url: `/api/users/${state.id}`,
          decoder: userDecoder,
          toMsg: (r) => r.tag === "Ok"
            ? { tag: "GotUser", user: r.value }
            : { tag: "FetchFailed", error: r.error },
        }),
      );

    case "Save":
      return withFx(state,
        storageSet("data", JSON.stringify(state), (r) =>
          r.tag === "Ok" ? { tag: "Saved" } : { tag: "SaveFailed", error: r.error }),
      );

    case "Load":
      return withFx(state,
        storageGet({
          key: "data",
          decoder: Decode.object({ count: Decode.number }),
          toMsg: (r) => r.tag === "Ok"
            ? { tag: "Loaded", value: r.value }   // r.value: T | undefined
            : { tag: "LoadFailed", error: r.error },
        }),
      );

    default:
      return noFx(state);
  }
}

--- Pattern matching on HttpError ---

import { HttpError } from "teelm/functional";

case "FetchFailed": {
  const e = msg.error;
  switch (e.tag) {
    case "BadUrl":       return noFx({ ...state, message: `Bad URL: ${e.url}` });
    case "Timeout":      return noFx({ ...state, message: "Request timed out" });
    case "NetworkError": return noFx({ ...state, message: e.message });
    case "BadStatus":    return noFx({ ...state, message: `HTTP ${e.status}` });
    case "BadBody":      return noFx({ ...state, message: `Decode failed: ${e.reason}` });
  }
}

--- Conditional subscriptions ---

import { interval, onKeyDown, websocket } from "teelm/subs";
import type { Subs } from "teelm";

function subscriptions(state: State): Subs<Msg> {
  return [
    onKeyDown((key) => ({ tag: "Key", key })),
    state.auto && interval(1000, { tag: "Tick" }),
    state.wsEnabled && websocket({
      url: state.wsUrl,
      onMessage: (data) => ({ tag: "WsMsg", data }),
    }),
  ];
}

--- Init with effects ---

const init: Init<State, Msg> = [
  { items: [], loading: true },
  // batch lifts a list of Effect<Msg> into a Cmd<Msg>:
  batch([
    [http({ url: "/api/items", decoder: itemsDecoder,
            toMsg: (r) => r.tag === "Ok" ? { tag: "Got", items: r.value } : { tag: "Err", e: r.error } })] as Cmd<Msg>,
  ]),
];

// Or, more idiomatically, use withFx:
import { withFx } from "teelm";
const init2: Init<State, Msg> = withFx(
  { items: [], loading: true },
  http({
    url: "/api/items",
    decoder: itemsDecoder,
    toMsg: (r) => r.tag === "Ok" ? { tag: "Got", items: r.value } : { tag: "Err", e: r.error },
  }),
);

--- Nested TEA composition ---

import { mapDispatch, mapCmd } from "teelm";
import * as Counter from "./counter";

type Msg = { tag: "Child"; msg: Counter.Msg };

// In view:
Counter.view(
  state.child,
  mapDispatch(dispatch, (m: Counter.Msg): Msg => ({ tag: "Child", msg: m })),
);

// In update — lift the child's Cmd:
case "Child": {
  const [childState, childCmd] = Counter.update(state.child, msg.msg);
  return [
    { ...state, child: childState },
    mapCmd(childCmd, (m): Msg => ({ tag: "Child", msg: m })),
  ];
}

// In subscriptions — lift each child sub:
function subscriptions(state: State): Subs<Msg> {
  return Counter.subscriptions(state.child).map((s) =>
    mapSub(s, (m: Counter.Msg): Msg => ({ tag: "Child", msg: m })),
  );
}

--- Router page with fetch and caching ---

import { noFx, withFx, h, type Dispatch } from "teelm";
import { http } from "teelm/fx";
import { Decode } from "teelm/functional";
import { type PageConfig } from "teelm/router";
import type { Shared } from "../shared";

interface User { id: number; name: string }
interface Model { id: number; user: User | null; loading: boolean; error: string | null }
type Msg =
  | { tag: "GotUser"; user: User }
  | { tag: "FetchFailed"; error: string };

const userDecoder = Decode.object({ id: Decode.number, name: Decode.string });

export const page: PageConfig<Model, Msg, Shared, { id: number }> = {
  init: (params) => withFx(
    { id: params.id, user: null, loading: true, error: null },
    http({
      url: `/api/users/${params.id}`,
      decoder: userDecoder,
      toMsg: (r) => r.tag === "Ok"
        ? { tag: "GotUser", user: r.value }
        : { tag: "FetchFailed", error: HttpError.toString(r.error) },
    }),
  ),

  update: (model, msg) => {
    switch (msg.tag) {
      case "GotUser":     return noFx({ ...model, user: msg.user, loading: false });
      case "FetchFailed": return noFx({ ...model, error: msg.error, loading: false });
    }
  },

  view: (model) => {
    if (model.loading) return h("p", {}, "Loading…");
    if (model.error)   return h("p", { class: "error" }, model.error);
    return h("h1", {}, model.user!.name);
  },

  save: (model) => model,
  load: (saved) => noFx(saved as Model),
};

--- Page with guard ---

page(route("/admin"), adminPage, {
  guard: (params, shared) => shared.user?.isAdmin === true ? true : "/login",
})

--- Tasks for chained async ---

import { Task } from "teelm/task";
import { withFx } from "teelm";

const fetchAuth = Task.fromPromise<AuthFailed, { token: string }>(
  () => fetch("/api/auth").then(r => r.json()),
  (e) => ({ tag: "AuthFailed", message: String(e) }),
);

const fetchProfile = (token: string) => Task.fromPromise<ProfileFailed, Profile>(
  () => fetch(`/api/me?t=${token}`).then(r => r.json()),
  (e) => ({ tag: "ProfileFailed", message: String(e) }),
);

const loadProfile = Task.andThen(
  Task.mapError(fetchAuth, (e): AppError => ({ kind: "auth", error: e })),
  ({ token }) => Task.mapError(fetchProfile(token), (e): AppError => ({ kind: "profile", error: e })),
);

case "Boot":
  return withFx(state, Task.attempt(loadProfile, (r) => ({ tag: "ProfileLoaded", r })));

--- Typed events ---

import { makeEvents } from "teelm/events";

function view(state: State, dispatch: Dispatch<Msg>) {
  const E = makeEvents(dispatch);
  return h("form", E.onSubmit({ tag: "Save" }),
    h("input", {
      type: "text",
      value: state.name,
      ...E.onInput((value) => ({ tag: "SetName", value })),
      ...E.onEnter({ tag: "Save" }),
    }),
    h("label", {},
      h("input", { type: "checkbox", checked: state.opt, ...E.onChecked((c) => ({ tag: "ToggleOpt", on: c })) }),
      h("span", {}, "Opt-in"),
    ),
    h("button", { type: "submit" }, "Save"),
  );
}

--- Custom effect (branding via `as Effect<Msg>` is not needed — Effect is unbranded) ---

import type { Dispatch, Effect } from "teelm";

function clipboardWriteFx<Msg>(
  dispatch: Dispatch<Msg>,
  props: { text: string; toMsg: (r: Result<undefined, string>) => Msg },
): void {
  navigator.clipboard.writeText(props.text)
    .then(() => dispatch(props.toMsg(Result.ok(undefined))))
    .catch((e) => dispatch(props.toMsg(Result.err(String(e)))));
}

export function clipboardWrite<Msg>(
  text: string,
  toMsg: (r: Result<undefined, string>) => Msg,
): Effect<Msg, { text: string; toMsg: (r: Result<undefined, string>) => Msg }> {
  return [clipboardWriteFx, { text, toMsg }];
}

--- Custom subscription (Sub<Msg> is branded — use the helper pattern) ---

import type { Dispatch, Sub, SubFn } from "teelm";

function visibilitySub<Msg>(
  dispatch: Dispatch<Msg>,
  props: { msg: (visible: boolean) => Msg },
): () => void {
  const handler = () => dispatch(props.msg(!document.hidden));
  document.addEventListener("visibilitychange", handler);
  return () => document.removeEventListener("visibilitychange", handler);
}

// Cast to Sub<Msg, P> — the only way to brand a tuple as a Sub.
export function onVisibilityChange<Msg>(
  msg: (visible: boolean) => Msg,
): Sub<Msg, { msg: (visible: boolean) => Msg }> {
  return [visibilitySub as SubFn<Msg, { msg: (visible: boolean) => Msg }>, { msg }] as unknown as Sub<Msg, { msg: (visible: boolean) => Msg }>;
}

--- Class prop variants ---

h("div", { class: "foo bar" })
h("div", { class: ["foo", condition && "bar"] })
h("div", { class: { foo: true, bar: isActive } })

--- Keyed list rendering ---

h("ul", {},
  ...state.items.map((item) =>
    h("li", { key: item.id }, item.name),
  ),
)

================================================================================
CLI COMMANDS
================================================================================

teelm new <name> [--jsx]
  Scaffold new project. Generates: index.html, package.json, tsconfig.json,
  vite.config.ts, src/main.ts(x), src/shared.ts, src/app.css,
  src/pages/{Home,About,NotFound}.ts(x), src/generated/router.ts.

teelm add <pattern> [--jsx]
  Add a page file under src/pages/ and run gen.
    "About"              → src/pages/About.ts,         route /about
    "Home"               → src/pages/Home.ts,          route /
    "NotFound"           → src/pages/NotFound.ts,      404 handler
    "users/[id]"         → src/pages/users/[id].ts,    /users/:id (string)
    "users/[id:int]"     → src/pages/users/[id:int].ts /users/:id (int)
    "users/[id:float]"   → src/pages/users/[id:float].ts /users/:id (float)
    "users/[id]/Edit"    → src/pages/users/[id]/Edit.ts /users/:id/edit
    "Blog/Index"         → src/pages/Blog/Index.ts     /blog

  Generated page scaffold uses tuple-only init/update:
    export const page: PageConfig<{}, never, Shared, {}> = {
      init: () => noFx({}),
      update: (model) => noFx(model),
      view: (model, shared) => h("div", { class: "..." }, h("h1", {}, "/about")),
    };

teelm gen
  Scan src/pages/, regenerate src/generated/router.ts.
  Sort: static before dynamic, more segments first, alphabetical tiebreak.

teelm dev
  Run gen, then start `npx vite` (HMR).

teelm build
  Run gen, then run `npx vite build`.

================================================================================
FILE CONVENTIONS FOR PAGES
================================================================================

Directory: src/pages/
Each page must export:  export const page: PageConfig<Model, Msg, Shared, Params>

File naming → route:
  Home.ts            → /                  (special)
  About.ts           → /about
  NotFound.ts        → 404 handler         (special)
  UserProfile.ts     → /user-profile       (CamelCase → kebab-case)
  Blog/Index.ts      → /blog               (Index maps to parent)
  users/Index.ts     → /users
  users/[id].ts      → /users/:id          (string)
  users/[id:int].ts  → /users/:id          (integer)
  users/[id:float].ts→ /users/:id          (float)
  [slug].ts          → /:slug

Ignored by `teelm gen`:
  - files and folders prefixed with `_`
  - *.component.ts(x)
  - *.test.ts(x), *.spec.ts(x), *.d.ts
  - patterns from project-root .teelmignore
  - lowercase index.ts(x) is supported as an entrypoint

Generated router imports:
  from "../shared":  Shared (type) and initialShared (value)

================================================================================
COMMON MISTAKES AND FIXES
================================================================================

MISTAKE: Returning a bare state from update or init
  return { ...state, count: state.count + 1 };  // type error: not a tuple
FIX: Always wrap with noFx or withFx
  return noFx({ ...state, count: state.count + 1 });

MISTAKE: Forgetting to return both branches of a switch via noFx/withFx
  case "Inc": return noFx({ ...state, count: state.count + 1 });
  case "FetchUser":
    return { ...state, loading: true };  // type error
FIX:
  case "FetchUser":
    return withFx({ ...state, loading: true }, http({ ... }));

MISTAKE: Using a raw [fn, props] tuple where Sub<Msg> is required
  subscriptions: () => [[myRunner, { ms: 1000 }]]   // type error: not branded
FIX: Use a sub creator from teelm/subs, or build your own helper that
  casts through Sub<Msg, P> (see "Custom subscription" pattern). Same goes
  for Cmd<Msg> — only the public helpers (none, withFx, batch, mapCmd,
  mapEffect) produce a value of that type.

MISTAKE: Typing subscriptions as Sub<Msg>[]
  function subscriptions(s: State): Sub<Msg>[] { ... }   // wrong
FIX: Use Subs<Msg>, which permits falsy entries:
  function subscriptions(s: State): Subs<Msg> { return [s.auto && interval(...), ...]; }

MISTAKE: Returning null from update to "destroy" the app
  if (msg.tag === "Quit") return null;   // not supported
FIX: Call instance.destroy() on the AppInstance returned from app().

MISTAKE: Using the old http() onOk/onError shape
  http({ url, onOk: (d) => ..., onError: (e) => ... })   // type error
FIX: Use decoder + toMsg with a Result<T, HttpError>:
  http({
    url,
    decoder: Decode.object({ ... }),
    toMsg: (r) => r.tag === "Ok" ? { tag: "Got", v: r.value } : { tag: "Err", e: r.error },
  })

MISTAKE: Using the old storageGet(key, callback) shape
  storageGet("k", (raw) => ({ tag: "Got", raw }))        // type error
FIX:
  storageGet({
    key: "k",
    decoder: Decode.string,
    toMsg: (r) => r.tag === "Ok" ? { tag: "Got", value: r.value } : { tag: "Err", e: r.error },
  })

MISTAKE: Mutating state directly
  state.count += 1;   // throws — state is deep-frozen
FIX: Always return a new object:
  return noFx({ ...state, count: state.count + 1 });

MISTAKE: Subscription order changes cause unintended teardown
  Reordering subscriptions in the array tears down + restarts existing subs.
FIX: Keep positions stable. Use falsy entries (`cond && interval(...)`) to
  toggle subs in place rather than splicing them in/out.

MISTAKE: Using onUrlChange with routerApp
  routerApp already listens via router.listen(); double-listening dispatches twice.
FIX: Only use onUrlChange for manual setups (no routerApp).

MISTAKE: Editing src/generated/router.ts manually
  It is overwritten by `teelm gen`.
FIX: Edit page files in src/pages/ and re-run gen.

MISTAKE: Page file uses default export
  export default { init, update, view };   // not picked up
FIX: Named export:
  export const page: PageConfig<...> = { init, update, view };

MISTAKE: Guard returning a boolean
  guard: (params, shared) => shared.isAdmin   // returns boolean
FIX: Return true OR a redirect URL string:
  guard: (p, s) => s.isAdmin ? true : "/login"

MISTAKE: Infinite guard redirect loop
  Guards chain at most 5 levels before falling through to notFound.
FIX: Design guard logic so cycles are impossible.

MISTAKE: Not spreading routerLink
  h("a", { routerLink("/about") })   // wrong — routerLink returns an object
FIX:
  h("a", { ...routerLink("/about") }, "About")

MISTAKE: Building a Decoder<T> by hand for everything
  Decoders compose. Prefer Decode.object / Decode.array / Decode.field /
  Decode.optional / Decode.oneOf / Decode.map / Decode.andThen over inlining
  manual checks.
